focus: Hallmark color, typography, copy, imagery, custom direction, hero craft, floating navigation, and responsive quality versus shipped Interface and Foundations assets
noveltyJustification: Six rows establish fully new file-level gaps and three refine prior inventory or responsive overlap into exact existing-file changes; (6 + 3*0.5) / 9 = 0.83.
keyQuestions: Which of the nine named Hallmark assets adds a real capability beyond shipped Interface and Foundations guidance?
What exact existing file should own each useful rule without adding commands or presets?
Which reuse choices require Hallmark notice or separate third-party rights handling?
answeredQuestions: All nine named Hallmark assets now have exact target files, a COPY/ADAPT/LEARN/INSPIRE-NEW/SKIP verdict, a surgical change, value, effort, and licensing treatment.
The highest-value gaps are signature-role hero selection, validated custom-craft evidence, fallback font metrics, authored-direction depth, and target-derived responsive proof.
ruledOut: Copying Hallmark fixed palettes, font catalogs, theme catalog, craft recipes, hero archetype catalog, or floating-nav timing values
Treating fixed 320/375/414/768 widths as universal breakpoints
Hotlinking or redistributing the Hallmark imagery kit
Adding a standalone redesign, variant, theme, craft, hero, or floating-nav command

change: Require canonical palette and semantic role tokens to be opaque; model alpha only in explicitly named overlay, scrim, shadow, and optical-separator roles.
licensing: Independently restate the role rule; no notice. Copying Hallmark prose or palette recipes requires MIT notice.

change: Add pairing and loading proof fields for display/body/utility role, source and rights, loaded weights, fallback family, size-adjust, ascent-override, descent-override, line-gap-override, and fallback-versus-loaded layout shift.
licensing: Native schema extension; do not copy the font catalog or fixed tone pairings.

change: Add latency-aware loading copy: silent for genuinely transient waits, operation label for perceptible waits, and real progress or an honest expectation for long waits; derive thresholds from measured product latency.
licensing: Idea-level rewrite needs no notice; do not copy attributed voice samples because Hallmark cannot establish quotation redistribution rights.

change: Add an optional owned-asset manifest inside the existing grounding/handoff envelope with stable owned URI, rights, attribution, dimensions, format, palette fit, intended role, checksum, fallback, and theme/mobile treatment.
licensing: New native schema only. Never copy, hotlink, or redistribute Hallmark-hosted binaries; repository MIT does not establish those asset rights.

change: Classify direction as precedent-grounded, authored-tuned, or authored-bespoke. Use tuned only for explicit brand tokens or unsupported multi-attribute direction, bespoke only when composition itself is the ask; preserve owned systems, label authored values, preview palette/type/structure, and retain the same proof floor.
licensing: Independently worded routing; no catalog, axis names, stamps, or worked palettes copied.

change: Replace the blanket hand-built-SVG prohibition with a ban on unvalidated fallback art. Permit brief-grounded CSS/SVG only with purpose, reuse search, provenance, semantic labeling, bundle cost, fallback, reduced-motion behavior, and render proof.
licensing: Adapt the evidence contract only; do not copy recipes, vendor claims, support percentages, or fixed budgets.

change: Replace mandatory hero/page imagery with a signature-role decision: typography, owned media, real product proof, generated asset, or explicit placeholder. When media exists require source, subject, crop/aspect, mobile transformation, load priority, alt/captions, and reduced-motion behavior; typography-only passes when brief-grounded.
licensing: Native correction with independent wording; do not import the eight-archetype catalog.

change: Add a stateful morph proof row: one semantic DOM owner, invariant layout geometry, explicit per-state property ownership, transform-only visual offset, one timing family, keyboard/focus behavior, reduced motion, and scroll performance evidence.
licensing: Learn the invariant/property-ownership method; do not copy exact CSS, 520 ms timing, threshold, or shadow recipe.

change: Add a target-derived verification matrix covering minimum supported width, every content transition, one intermediate width, zoom/text expansion, portrait/landscape, pointer/hover, safe areas, horizontal overflow, unbreakable content, image-track zero minimums, wrapped affordances, and focus-induced scroll jumps.
licensing: Independently worded proof matrix; Hallmark widths may appear only as a labeled marketing-web example, never doctrine.

reason: Copying Hallmark fixed palettes, font catalogs, theme catalog, craft recipes, hero archetype catalog, or floating-nav timing values
Treating fixed 320/375/414/768 widths as universal breakpoints
Hotlinking or redistributing the Hallmark imagery kit
Adding a standalone redesign, variant, theme, craft, hero, or floating-nav command
